parameters: \n[1]. You're not making trouble for the Kurumizawas, are you?
I think you should learn how to cook a bit, so that you can reduce
Just as I was trying to get something from the boxes past my father's
door, I unluckily ended up running into him.
With how soundproofed his room is, it's hard to detect his presence.
Normally I wouldn't mind that, but it's really troublesome
The irritation within me grew, as he nagged on and on.
...Especially since I knew that what he was saying wasn't wrong.
\SE[2]Oi, are you listening? I don't really want to say this myself either,
but it's for your own good, you know.
...It's been more than a decade since then. And of course I realize
that the words he said back then were probably just a lie.
But, the memory and impact of that moment was still buried
deep within my core, without disappearing.
--I wonder if this is what you call this a rebellious phase?
I don't really want to say this but...
If it wasn't for mom, I wouldn't mind listening to you either.
If it wasn't for mom, I wouldn't mind listening to you either.
I replied to my father, with a sharp tongue.
...I see, sorry. Forget what I said...
My father hung his head, with a regrettable, apologetic expression.
It was a phrase that was like magic... always instantly silencing my father.
A phone suddenly rang. Seemed like it was my father's.
He took it in his hand, and returned to his room as I watched on.
--I know things can't continue like this.
I wonder if even this is something that time will fix?
Or maybe we might just stay like this forever. I couldn't tell.
Back in the day, my father once said how he got the room soundproofed so that I wouldn't be

bothered studying for my college entrance exams. Not the faintest of sound could be heard 
from the inside.
As if to escape the indescribable guilt I felt, I ran away to my room
and wasted my time playing with my phone.
Would you like to view [Shino's viewpoint]?
(You can view it at any time from the Room of Recollections.)
This is my old man's room.
...You're helping us out a lot... so you really don't...
